cases.title:
マネタイズ案（初心者でも明日から狙える具体例）
1.  TikTok/YouTube Shorts爆速量産 刺激的なサムネ効果抜群のショート動画を1日数十本自動生成 → 再生回数でアド収益化（規約ギリギリラインは自己責任で）
2.  ストックビデオ販売 Pond5、Shutterstock、Adobe Stockなどに「高品質リアル動画」を大量アップロード。無修正だから競合が少ないニッチ需要を独占
cases.content:
マネタイズ案（初心者でも明日から狙える具体例）
1.  TikTok/YouTube Shorts爆速量産 刺激的なサムネ効果抜群のショート動画を1日数十本自動生成 → 再生回数でアド収益化（規約ギリギリラインは自己責任で）
2.  ストックビデオ販売 Pond5、Shutterstock、Adobe Stockなどに「高品質リアル動画」を大量アップロード。無修正だから競合が少ないニッチ需要を独占可能。
3.  成人向けコンテンツ制作 OnlyFans、Fantia、Patreonなどで「無修正動画」を武器にファン課金モデル。ローカル生成なのでプラットフォームにBANされにくい。
4.  フリーランス動画制作受注 企業向け「ちょっと刺激的なプロモ動画」やクリエイター向け「特殊効果動画」をクラウドワークスやココナラで受注。単価3〜10万円クラスも狙える。
5.  派生ビジネス Sulphur 2をさらにファインチューニングした自分専用モデルを作成 → 「無修正特化モデル販売」や「生成代行サービス」としてマネタイズ。

基本的にガチ恋だから贈るという

LIVE配信っていうだけあって

みたいな感覚と近いのかなって！

良かった！とか感動した！とか
楽しかったとかの気持ちに応じた
ひとつの形だと思ってるよ👍

※もちろん中にはガチ恋だから！
という方もいると思うし
そういう配信者の方もいるとおもう！

✦ 応援
✦ 感謝
✦ 感動
✦ 楽しかった
✦ 鑑賞チケット感覚
✦ その場のノリ

このあたりが多いんじゃないかな？
その場のノリでうん〇ステッカーは
最近うちでは多いかもｗｗｗ

あとよく
歌ならライブチケット代のかわりに！
参加型で楽しかった参加費に！
とかで贈ってくれる子も多いかな！

なのでぼくにガチ恋だから
投げてるんだ！って思うのは
不憫に思ってしまて😹ｗ

で、ぼくは感謝の気持ちを
ThanksCardという形で月1だけど
お礼させていただいております🙏

配信って配信者だけじゃなくて
リスナーさんありきなので

すぐ伝えるようにはしているよ🐺

配信者としてのぼくからの意見も
伝えておきたかったのでポストしました🙏

もちろん人によってこの意見は違うと
思うので、あくまでぼくの意見です✋

とは思ってます₍ᐢ ⸝⸝- ̫ -⸝⸝ ᐢ₎

cases.title:
🚨 ニキータ・ビア（X製品責任者）が本気で狙っていること
MrBeastみたいな巨大クリエイターの動画がYouTubeから勝手にリッピングされてXに上がっても、最初にXに自分で投稿した人が収益を取れる仕組みを今、内部テスト中。
・Xが動画の重複検出を強化
・投稿順で「オリジナル」を判定
・後から上がったリポストや他人のアップロードは、オリジナル投稿者にインプレッション・収益を帰属させる方向
つまり
cases.content:
🚨 ニキータ・ビア（X製品責任者）が本気で狙っていること
MrBeastみたいな巨大クリエイターの動画がYouTubeから勝手にリッピングされてXに上がっても、最初にXに自分で投稿した人が収益を取れる仕組みを今、内部テスト中。
・Xが動画の重複検出を強化
・投稿順で「オリジナル」を判定
・後から上がったリポストや他人のアップロードは、オリジナル投稿者にインプレッション・収益を帰属させる方向
つまり「YouTubeのクリップをただ貼るだけ」じゃなく、Xに一番乗りでオリジナル投稿した人が得をする世界にしようとしてる。
これでクリエイターに「Xを第一投稿プラットフォームにしろ」と強力なインセンティブを与える狙い。
YouTubeの二次利用を減らし、Xに良質な長尺・オリジナル動画を呼び込みたいんだと思います。
クリエイターは今のうちにXを本気で使った方が絶対に得しそうですね。

cases.content:
baby boomers control 70% of all disposable income in the US

average net worth at 55-64: $1.57 million. at 65-74: $1.78 million.

and virtually, there's a small circle of people building AI content that targets them.

most are fighting over 22 year olds who spend $4.99 on a digital product after watching 47 videos about it

the psychology is brutal. boomers don't scroll tiktok ironically.

they're on facebook and youtube watching 8 minute videos about joint pain and retirement planning and they BUY. not "add to cart and abandon." buy.

with real credit cards attached to checking accounts that have actual money in them

meanwhile you're making looksmaxxing slideshows for teenagers who can't afford a gym membership

here's what a boomer-focused ai content page looks like:

> pick a health or finance niche they care about. menopause, sleep, knee pain, social security timing, downsizing
> build a warm, trustworthy ai character (think: retired nurse, financial planner energy... NOT hustle bro)
> post on youtube and facebook. not tiktok. they're not there
affiliate offers in the $49-197 range convert like crazy because $97 is nothing when you're sitting on $1.7M

(a guy i was talking to last month has a faceless "retirement wellness" youtube channel doing $11,400/mo in affiliate revenue. 14 months old. he posts 3x a week and doesn't show his face)

the reason nobody's doing this is ego. building content for 60 year old women with hot flashes isn't sexy on twitter.

but those women have more buying power than your entire follower list combined...

cases.title:
YouTube doesn't pay everyone the same.

Two channels can get the exact same views and one makes 10x more than the other. 

The reason why is something nobody explains when they tell you to “start a Yo
cases.content:
YouTube doesn't pay everyone the same.

Two channels can get the exact same views and one makes 10x more than the other. 

The reason why is something nobody explains when they tell you to “start a YouTube channel.":

YouTube keeps 45% of ad revenue. Creators get 55%.

That 55% gets paid based on something called RPM (revenue per 1,000 views).

RPM varies wildly by niche:

• Gaming and entertainment: $2-5
• Lifestyle and vlogs: $3-8
• Finance and investing: $10-20
• Business breakdowns: $8-15
• Tech reviews: $5-12

The niche you pick basically decides your income before you even post a video.

A finance video with 100K views at $15 RPM pays $1,500.

A gaming video with 100K views at $3 RPM pays $300.

Same views. 5x the money.

This is why niche selection is the single most important decision. 

Not your editing, thumbnail, or posting schedule.

It’s the niche.

YouTube generated over $60 billion in revenue in 2025. 

That’s more than Netflix.

They have 2.5 billion monthly active users and they're actively pushing content to new viewers through their recommendation algorithm.

That means you don't need followers. 

YouTube puts your video in front of people who have never heard of you. 

If the content is good and the niche has demand, the views come.

Here's what most people miss about monetization:

You need 4,000 watch hours and 1,000 subscribers. That takes 60-90 days of posting 3 videos per week.

Your first month of monetized earnings will probably be $100-500. 

That sounds small…

But those videos keep paying you. 

Every single month. 

I have videos from 2022 that still generate revenue today. The content compounds.

This is also why channels are sellable assets.

YouTube channel sales jumped 155% on Flippa last year. 

Buyers pay 24-36x monthly profit because they're buying a revenue stream, not just a brand.

A channel making $5K/month is worth $120K-180K as an asset.

Most people don't even know this market exists. 

I've sold 3 channels for $100K+, $150K, and $40K.

And here's the part nobody talks about:

You don't need to be on camera to do any of this.

• I've built 19 faceless channels. 
• AI handles scripts and voiceovers. 
• Editors on Upwork handles the rest. 

Total cost: $50-70 per video.

The creator economy is worth $181.8 billion. YouTube is the biggest piece of it. And AI just made entry cheaper than ever.

I've worked with 4,000+ people building this through Growth Channels

DM me "YOUTUBE" and I'll show you how we can help you build this

& Follow me @razvanparaschiv for more content like this

cases.title:
I'm about to tell you something that's going to piss you off.

Not because it's harsh. 

But because you'll realize how much time you've wasted.

Right now there's a skill that takes 90 days to learn
cases.content:
I'm about to tell you something that's going to piss you off.

Not because it's harsh. 

But because you'll realize how much time you've wasted.

Right now there's a skill that takes 90 days to learn and will have brands throwing $5K-$10K/month at you. 

And almost nobody is learning it.

Instead you're probably watching dropshipping videos, learning how to automate things with AI, trying to build a faceless YouTube channel, or some other bullshit that sounds easy but leads nowhere.

Meanwhile ecom brands doing $100K+/month are literally desperate to find people who can do ONE thing:

Make ads that actually sell products.

Not viral TikToks. Not aesthetic reels. Not pretty brand videos. 

Ads that convert cold traffic into buyers.

Every successful ecom brand has the same problem. 

They need to test 50-100 new ads every single month to keep scaling. Ad fatigue is real. 

What works today stops working in 3-4 weeks. 

The only way to keep growing is to constantly pump out new creative that converts.

But nobody knows how to make ads that actually sell.

The market is flooded with video editors who can make things look pretty. 

Cool transitions. Motion graphics. Color grading. All that surface level shit. 

But they have zero understanding of what makes someone pull out their credit card. 

They don't know copywriting. They don't know marketing psychology. They don't know how to hook attention and drive a sale.

A pretty video that gets 0 sales is worth exactly $0.

Brand owners don't give a fuck about how aesthetic your editing is. 

They care about ROAS. Revenue. Money. 

They need ads that print cash. And almost nobody can deliver that.

I'm connected to hundreds of brand owners. 

I run a marketing agency. And I see this firsthand every single day. Brands are begging for help. 

Offering $3K, $5K, $8K, $10K/month retainers. Revenue share deals. 

And they can't find anyone.

The craziest part? 

The skill gap isn't even that big.

You don't need a marketing degree or 10 years of experience. 

Just basic video editing, an understanding of marketing psychology and what triggers purchases, and knowing how to structure an ad that converts cold traffic into buyers. 

Stack those three things and you're suddenly worth $5K-$10K/month to brands.

Nobody's doing this because it's not sexy. 

It's not passive income while you sleep. It's not traveling Bali while your laptop makes you money. 

It's an actual skill that requires actual work to learn. 

And most people online are allergic to that.

They want shortcuts. They want easy. They want fast.

So they keep bouncing from one shiny object to the next, never building anything real. 

Meanwhile the people who just shut the fuck up and learn how to make ads are quietly making $10K/month working with brands who actually have budgets.

This isn't going away either. 

Ecom brands will always need ads. 

As long as people are buying products online, brands will need creative that converts. 

The demand isn't going anywhere.

But the window where the competition is this low won't last forever. 

Right now if you're even decent at this you win. 

A year from now you'll need to be actually good to stand out.

The brands are there. The budgets are there. The demand is there.

The only question is whether you're willing to stop chasing shortcuts and actually build a real skill.

Think about this for a second.

cases.content:
This was four years ago when AI wasn’t even in the picture. 

You would go on Google and Wikipedia, research celebrities, and turn that into video content. 

Where did you expect people to get AI voices from? The ones available back then were very expensive. I did it the hard way. 

The main reason I lost monetization on that channel is because I used the same AdSense for blogging, and the channel is still there today. 

It’s even tied to my personal email for blogging.

Before I built this, I created many projects that I later sold because I’ve always believed I can start again from scratch. 

When I built and lost Mayortune’s momentum, I went back to blogging and did it again. Eventually, I felt SEO blogging wasn’t worth it for me anymore, so I left it.

When AI made things easier, I kept the same mindset. 

From my blogging days, I’ve always been someone who shares value. 

I just want people to figure things out and earn for themselves. 

I never sold any course on blogging, but I shared value every day on my Facebook. 

Even when I lost that account, I kept going. 

People called me names, said I was oversharing, but I just wanted to see others grow.

It used to be a community full of good vibes, unlike many others, no negativity. 

But when people who were desperate to become “banger boys” came in, toxicity followed. 

Many of you were never really into YouTube automation, you jumped in for quick money, and when it didn’t come, the negativity started spreading.

I haven’t slept for eight hours straight in a very long time, maybe all of this was needed. 

But one thing I won’t accept is being trolled on my own page. This is not a banger or trolling account. 

I share value, and people follow me because they find it useful.
